criteria.name: Hook strength
criteria.notes: Names a concrete tension ('three federal AI threads...don't add up') and hints at the missing governance option. Not a single named deadline in the hook itself, but the structural tension is specific and this-week.

criteria.name: Local relevance
criteria.notes: Every claim is named-AK: UAF, DIU, JBER, Clear SFS, Eielson, ANLC, Sullivan, Tobin, Alaska's grid. No imported stories.

criteria.name: Factual density
criteria.notes: Dates (May 8/9/13/29), counts (1,400 grants, 4,700 acres, 12 sites), named judge, named senators, named bases, named UAF center, minus 40 degrees. Specifics in nearly every sentence.

criteria.name: Source quality
criteria.notes: UAF-DIU triple-sourced; DOGE/NEH ruling has PBS+WaPo+Sun Star; ADN/DeMarban primary for AF data centers; Alaska Beacon for daybook. Two highest-leverage stories re-verified by orchestrator. All claims in post map to verified findings.

criteria.name: Voice match
criteria.notes: Hits the post_001 structural pattern: conventional read, structurally-false counter, lock-in moment, debatable close. 'That's the lock-in' echoes the anchor. Slight compression vs the cleaner section beats of the example.

criteria.name: Readability (mobile)
criteria.notes: ~360 words, in target band. Paragraph 4 (the McMahon ruling) is dense for mobile and runs long; otherwise scannable.

criteria.name: Engagement question
criteria.notes: Real, debatable, and tied directly to the host-vs-governance tension that organizes the post. No follow-us, no hashtags.

one_sentence_fix: Split the long McMahon-ruling paragraph after the judge's quote so the ANLC sentence stands alone for mobile scanning.
